news.summary: The Federal Reserve decided to maintain interest rates at 4.25%-4.5% despite pressure for a decrease. Analyst Louis Navellier predicts the Fed will be one of the last central banks to cut rates, with expectations of four cuts this year. Other central banks like the Bank of England are cutting rates, prompting Navellier to believe that money will flow back to America due to higher rates. Navellier also noted progress in U.S.-China trade tensions. The Bank of England did cut its rates to 4.25% as expected. Navellier's full insights can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Federal Reserve holds rates at 4.25%-4.5% despite pressure. Expert predicts 4 cuts this year, citing global rate collapse. Fed expected to be last to cut.

news.summary: In Riverwoods, Illinois, Diners Club International® revealed a generous contribution to World Central Kitchen by donating $750,000. This donation aims to support communities affected by natural disasters and humanitarian crises globally. On May 7, 2025, for each transaction using a Diners Club card worldwide, the company pledged to provide one meal, with a cap of $750,000. Diners Club Members globally successfully reached the maximum donation limit, showcasing a fruitful donation initiative. The $750,000 donation from Diners Club to World Central Kitchen will play a crucial role in providing aid.
news.headline: Diners Club International® donates $750,000 to World Central Kitchen for disaster relief, funded through purchases on May 7, 2025.

news.summary: Atlanta-based restaurant chain, Huddle House, is revamping its brand to enhance customer experience. With James O’Reilly as CEO and Blain Shortreed as COO, the company is introducing a new brand identity after conducting extensive guest research. The move comes after 60 years of serving freshly prepared, home-style dishes. The reimagined brand will focus on improving core product quality and menu innovation. This transformation aims to cater better to its loyal customers and adapt to changing consumer preferences.
news.headline: Huddle House is renovating its brand to cater to new guests, focusing on new menu items and brand identity.

news.summary: A report on the Saudi Arabia Milk Powder market has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings, indicating significant growth trends. The market is projected to increase from US$401.4 million in 2024 to US$699.17 million by 2033, representing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 6.36% during the period from 2025 to 2033. This growth is attributed to factors such as rising demand for infant formula, expanding retail channels, governmental support for food security, heightened health awareness, and evolving consumer preferences.
news.headline: Saudi Arabia's milk powder market to reach $699.17 million by 2033 from $401.4 million in 2024, with 6.36% CAGR. Growth driven by infant formula demand, retail expansion, and health awareness.

news.summary: After several weeks of negotiations, President Trump has praised the completion of a comprehensive trade agreement between the United States and the United Kingdom. The deal is expected to significantly benefit the UK economy, with projected trade numbers exceeding $200 billion annually. This development comes as a positive step for UK Prime Minister Starmer and marks a significant milestone in strengthening diplomatic and economic ties between the two nations.
news.headline: Trump praises 'full' US-UK trade deal, boosting Starmer post weeks of talks. - Source withholds details.

news.summary: Ether (ETH) is on the rise, breaking a downtrend since December to target $3,000. Ethereum's total value locked (TVL) has surged by 41% to $52.8 billion in 30 days, with daily transactions rising by 22% to 1.34 million, signaling a strong network recovery. ETH faces resistance at $2,100-$2,800 but is set to recover towards $3,000, backed by increasing network activity and positive technical indicators. Analysts predict a bullish reversal for ETH, targeting $2,500-$3,500. However, low transaction activity and net outflows in Ether ETFs may hamper the quick rise to $3,000. Readers are advised to conduct their research before making investment decisions.
news.headline: ETH price breaks downtrend, aiming for $3K. TVL up 41% to $52.8B in 30 days, daily transactions at 1.34M. Resistance at $2,100-$2,800.

news.summary: A recent report on the Spain Application Security Market reveals promising growth prospects, with an anticipated annual increase of 17.5%. By 2025, the market size is estimated to reach USD 601.4 million. Between 2020 and 2024, the sector displayed a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 1%. The report provides insights into market opportunities, future dynamics, expenditure analysis based on industry, security type, deployment methods, and enterprise sizes. This information has been made available through ResearchAndMarkets.com.
news.headline: Spain's application security market set to reach $601.4 million by 2025, growing at 17.5% annually. Business opportunities increasing.

news.summary: In a recent webinar hosted in Needham, Massachusetts, IDC discussed the repercussions of tariffs on the economy and global supply chain adjustments. The webinar also covered the revised global IT spending forecast. The webinar highlighted the importance of understanding the economic impact of tariffs, with experts shedding light on the shifts in global supply chains. This insightful session provided key insights into the State of the Market, emphasizing the need to adapt to the changing landscape of global trade.
news.headline: IDC downgrades global IT spending forecast due to tariff uncertainty, now predicts growth between +5% and +9%.

news.summary: Dallas-based Sonida Senior Living, Inc. (NYSE: SNDA), a prominent senior living communities owner, operator, and investor, revealed today the participation of key executives in upcoming investor conferences. Brandon Ribar, the President & CEO, Kevin Detz, the CFO, and Max Levy, the CIO, will engage at the 2025 BMO North American Real Estate Conference in New York City. The event will include one-on-one meetings with Sonida management.
news.headline: Sonida Senior Living will participate in the 2025 BMO North American Real Estate Conference in NYC. CEO, CFO, and CIO will represent.

news.summary: A new study has found that over 70% of adults in the US have received at least one dose of the COVID-19 vaccine, with more than 50% now fully vaccinated. The research also showed that among adults aged 65 and older, nearly 90% have received at least one dose of the vaccine. These numbers indicate a significant increase in vaccination rates nationwide. Additionally, the study revealed that the Pfizer-BioNTech vaccine was the most commonly administered, followed by Moderna and Johnson & Johnson. This data underscores the progress being made in the country's vaccination efforts to combat the spread of the virus.
news.headline: SEC's new rule could cost Wall Street brokers billions in fees.
BoE Gov. Bailey: The financial system has come through market volatility robustly.

news.summary: ZenaTech, a technology company with a focus on AI drones, Drone as a Service, enterprise SaaS, and Quantum Computing, has revealed that its ZenaDrone subsidiary is currently trialing a groundbreaking specialized camera. This camera, created by the company's Taiwan component manufacturing branch, Spider Vision Sensors, in collaboration with Suntek Global, enhances indoor applications like inventory and security management when used with IQ Nano drone swarms for commercial and US defense purposes. The new camera promises quicker and more accurate data collection, enabling multiple drones within a swarm to simultaneously scan multiple bar codes. ZenaTech intends to seek Blue UAS certification for military and government drone employment, marking a significant move in the field.
news.headline: ZenaTech testing camera for IQ Nano drone swarms, aiming for Blue UAS certification; promises efficient data collection for inventory and security management indoors.

news.summary: Horsham, Pennsylvania based STRATA Skin Sciences, Inc. disclosed its participation in the D. Boral Capital Inaugural Global Conference on May 14, 2025. The company, traded on NASDAQ under SSKN, focuses on advancing products for treating dermatologic conditions. The event, located at The Plaza Hotel, 768 Fifth Avenue, New York, NY 10019, serves as a platform for STRATA to showcase its innovative medical technologies.
news.headline: STRATA Skin Sciences to join D. Boral Capital Global Conference on May 14, 2025 at The Plaza Hotel, New York.

news.summary: In South San Francisco, California, on May 8, 2025, CERo Therapeutics Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: CERO), a pioneering immunotherapy firm specializing in advancing engineered T cell therapeutics utilizing phagocytic processes, has successfully met the requirements of Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(b)(1). This regulation mandates a minimum stockholders' equity of $2.5 million, known as the "Equity Rule."
news.headline: CERo Therapeutics Holdings, Inc. complies with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(b)(1) with $2.5 million stockholders' equity.

news.summary: Semtech Corporation (NASDAQ: SMTC) based in Camarillo, California, a prominent supplier of high-performance semiconductor products, IoT systems, and cloud connectivity services, is set to present its innovative IoT solutions at the IoT Solutions World Congress (IOTSWC) scheduled to take place in Barcelona from May 13 to 15, 2025. In collaboration with the LoRa Alliance's initiative "Building Massive IoT with LoRaWAN," Semtech aims to exhibit the transformative impact of its latest LoRa technology, which marks the fourth generation, on connectivity.
news.headline: Semtech to showcase fourth-gen LoRa® tech at IoT Solutions World Congress in Barcelona, May 13-15, 2025.

news.summary: A partnership has been announced between Play2Pay and Metro by T-Mobile in Miami. Play2Pay is an innovative payment platform that adds a fun element to bill payments. Metro by T-Mobile, the largest prepaid brand in America, has collaborated with Play2Pay to introduce the Ad It Up app to its customers. This app allows customers, using iOS or Android devices, to play games and view promotions to accumulate rewards that can be utilized as credits towards their Metro service. By participating more, customers can earn more credits, leading to reduced payments for their wireless services. This collaboration aims to enhance customer engagement and provide added value to users.
news.headline: Play2Pay partners with Metro by T-Mobile to launch Ad It Up app, allowing customers to earn credits for wireless service by playing games & engaging with ads.

news.summary: Versa, a top player in Universal Secure Access Service Edge (SASE), has revealed that its VersaONE Universal SASE Platform has been recognized as a Leader and Outperformer in the GigaOm Radar Report for Security Service Edge (SSE). The report, from renowned technology research entity GigaOm, assessed 19 key security providers, categorizing them as Leaders, Challengers, or New Entrants according to their offerings. Versa's platform stood out in the evaluation, positioning it prominently in the industry.
news.headline: Versa named Leader and Outperformer in SSE, SD-WAN, and SASE by GigaOm. VersaONE platform ranked highly in report.

news.summary: WALTHAM, Mass.-- Evolv Technologies Holdings, Inc. revealed its collaboration with Rahway Public Schools in Rahway, NJ, involving the implementation of Evolv Express® and Evolv eXpedite™ systems at key entry points like the gymnasium and performing arts entrances. The use of these advanced AI-based solutions signifies a major advancement in school security for the district. The partnership announcement comes as Evolv Technologies Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: EVLV), a renowned security technology company, reinforces its commitment to enhancing safety measures and experiences within educational institutions.
news.headline: Rahway Public Schools partners with Evolv Technologies for safety, deploying Express and eXpedite systems for enhanced security.

news.summary: London - nVent, a prominent player in electrical connection and protection solutions, disclosed its involvement in the Wolfe Global Transportation and Industrials Conference scheduled for Thursday, May 22, 2025. The Chair and Chief Executive Officer Beth Wozniak will deliver a presentation at 2:35 p.m. Eastern Time. Those interested can access a webcast through nVent's Investor Relations website, available at https://investors.nvent.com/events-and-presentations. nVent is recognized as a top global vendor of electrical connections and protections.
news.headline: nVent Electric to join Wolfe Global Transportation and Industrials Conference on May 22, 2025. CEO to present at 2:35 pm ET. Visit nVent's IR website for webcast.

news.summary: In Chicago, the prominent cybersecurity platform for small and midsize businesses, Coro, introduced its Security Awareness Training (SAT) module. This specialized solution aims to assist SMBs in decreasing human errors, preventing phishing attacks, and showcasing compliance without requiring additional tools for management. Integrated within Coro’s comprehensive cybersecurity platform, the newly launched Security Awareness Training module offers heightened protection with simplified usability. Research shows that more than 90% of cyber breaches originate from human error, emphasizing the critical need for robust training mechanisms like Coro’s SAT module.
news.headline: Coro launches Security Awareness Training to help SMBs reduce human error and defend against phishing attacks.

Bill Gates pledged on Thursday to give away almost his entire personal wealth in the next two decades and said the world's poorest would receive some $200 billion via his foundation at a time when governments worldwide are slashing international aid..

news.summary: Brandon Perry has been appointed as the Vice President of Lending at the Texas Methodist Foundation, based in Austin, Texas.
news.headline: The Texas Methodist Foundation appoints Brandon Perry as Vice President of Lending in Austin.

news.summary: Liberty Mutual Holding Company Inc. reported a net income of $1.025 billion for the first quarter of 2025, a decrease from $1.535 billion in the same period last year. Despite California wildfires driving elevated catastrophe losses, their combined ratio, including these losses and prior year development, was 96.6%. They achieved an 81.9% underlying combined ratio, marking a 6.5-point improvement. Investment income contributed $1.3 billion, and overall, the company aims to reach a 95% combined ratio goal by the end of 2025. US Retail Markets' net written premium decreased by 7.4%, while Global Risk Solutions saw an increase of 7.5%. Consolidated revenues remained relatively stable at $12.486 billion. Underlying profits before limited partnerships income rose by 40.8% to $2.711 billion. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Liberty Mutual reported Q1 net income of $1.025 billion, down from $1.535 billion in 2024, achieved a 81.9% combined ratio.
